verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted CAP-family protein with no TM domain of its own. It is secreted by epididymal epithelium and associates with the sperm surface during epididymal maturation. Two populations exist: a loosely bound form (protein D) that maintains reversible equilibrium with the soluble pool and washes off (secreted-only behavior), and a tightly/firmly bound form (protein E) that persists on the sperm surface post-washing and behaves as an integral membrane protein. This wash-resistant surface pool is restricted entirely to the germline/gamete compartment (sperm), qualifying as contextual/tissue_restricted_surface. Outside this narrow reproductive cell-type lineage, CRISP1 is a secreted protein with no documented stable surface pool.
